news.title:
JK Lakshmi Cement focuses on capacity expansion to grow market share.
This move comes amid industry-wide capacity additions.
news.summary:

news.title:
JK Lakshmi Cement eyes eased supply chains via India-EU, US FTAs amid high pet coke costs.
Company targets net zero by 2047 with new LC3 cement, plans 5-6% CAGR for green products.
news.summary:
JK Lakshmi Cement eyes eased supply chains via India-EU, US FTAs amid high pet coke costs.
Company targets net zero by 2047 with new LC3 cement, plans 5-6% CAGR for green products.

news.title:
JK Lakshmi Cement forecasts improved EBITDA in Q4 due to higher sales.
President Arun Kumar Shukla cites rising sales realisation and growing B2B volumes.
news.summary:
JK Lakshmi Cement forecasts improved EBITDA in Q4 due to higher sales.

news.title:
Cement majors like Shree Cement, UltraTech, and Ambuja posted double-digit volume growth in Q3.
Firms expect demand and price recovery, aided by low inflation, tax reforms, and infrastructure growth.
news.summary:
Cement majors like Shree Cement, UltraTech, and Ambuja posted double-digit volume growth in Q3.

news.title:
Govt of Assam cancels limestone mining blocks awarded to JK Lakshmi Cement.
Cancellation may delay JK Lakshmi's cement project; company plans legal action.
news.summary:
Govt of Assam cancels limestone mining blocks awarded to JK Lakshmi Cement.
Cancellation may delay JK Lakshmi's cement project; company plans legal action.

news.title:
JK Lakshmi Cement's Q3 FY26 results discussed; trade sales dropped due to Surat station commissioning.
Capex for Durg Line 2 set at Rs 650 cr in FY26; completion by March 2028.
news.summary:
JK Lakshmi Cement's Q3 FY26 results discussed; trade sales dropped due to Surat station commissioning.
Capex for Durg Line 2 set at Rs 650 cr in FY26; completion by March 2028.

news.title:
Triveni Turbine, JK Lakshmi Cement, Mankind Pharma, Aarti Drugs, and Bharat Coking Coal saw losses up to 9% following weak Q3 results.
Triveni, JK Lakshmi, Mankind, Aarti, and Bharat Coking Coal reported margin contractions, profit declines, or lower realizations impacting market sentiments.
news.summary:
Triveni Turbine, JK Lakshmi Cement, Mankind Pharma, Aarti Drugs, and Bharat Coking Coal saw losses up to 9% following weak Q3 results.
Triveni, JK Lakshmi, Mankind, Aarti, and Bharat Coking Coal reported margin contractions, profit declines, or lower realizations impacting market sentiments.

news.title:
JK Lakshmi Cement approves revised expansion in Chhattisgarh, raising project costs from ₹2,500 Cr to ₹3,000 Cr.
Clinker capacity to rise to 12.3 MTPA and cement to 22.6 MTPA by March 2028.
news.summary:
JK Lakshmi Cement approves revised expansion in Chhattisgarh, raising project costs from ₹2,500 Cr to ₹3,000 Cr.
Clinker capacity to rise to 12.3 MTPA and cement to 22.6 MTPA by March 2028.

news.title:
JK Lakshmi Cement's Q3 net profit fell to 569M INR from 749M YoY.
Revenue rose to 15.88B INR in Q3 from 14.97B INR YoY.
news.summary:
JK Lakshmi Cement's Q3 net profit fell to 569M INR from 749M YoY.
Revenue rose to 15.88B INR in Q3 from 14.97B INR YoY.

news.title:
JK Lakshmi Cement to hold earnings call on 4th Feb 2026 at 4 PM IST.
PhillipCapital organizes the call to discuss Q3 and 9-month results ending Dec 2025.
news.summary:
JK Lakshmi Cement to hold earnings call on 4th Feb 2026 at 4 PM IST.

news.title:
Nuvoco, Birla, and JK Lakshmi Cement focus on premium products to sustain margins.
Analysts warn the strategy may fail long-term amid rising competition and GST changes.
news.summary:

news.title:

Analysis links these changes to operational performance and market environment.

news.title:
Bharti Airtel's ICIL to sell 0.56% stake for ₹806M at ₹2,096.70/share, 3% discount to last close.
JK Lakshmi Cement plans ₹1,816 crore investment to boost Chhattisgarh operations; AU Small Finance appoints 2 independent directors.
news.summary:

JK Lakshmi Cement plans ₹1,816 crore investment to boost Chhattisgarh operations; AU Small Finance appoints 2 independent directors.

news.title:
JK Lakshmi Cement aims 30 MT capacity by FY30, driven by Rs. 3,000 Cr Durg expansion.
New plants planned in Nagaur, Kutch, and Assam to achieve growth target.
news.summary:
